sutta: the buddha said monks possessed of 4 qualities a man is incapable of falling away he is near to nibana what are the 4 herein among is perfect in virtue he is guarded as to the doors of the sense faculties he is moderate in eating he is given to watchfulness or wakefulness and in what way is among perfect in virtue hearing among is virtuous he dwells restrained with the restraint of the patimoka the code of preception for among perfect in the practice of right conduct he sees danger in the slightest faults he takes up and trains himself in the stages of training thus among is perfect in virtue and how is a man guarded as to the doors of the sense faculties hearing among seeing an object with the eye does not grasp at the general features or at the details thereof since coveting and dejection evil and profitable states might flow in upon 1 who dwells with the faculty of the eye unrestrained he applies himself to such restraint he sets a guard over the faculty of the eye attains restrained thereof when he hears a sound with the ear or with the nose smells ascend or with the tongue stays a flavor or with the body contacts tangibles when with the mind he cognizes mental states he does not grasp at the general features or details thereof but since coveting and dejection evil and profitable states might flow in upon 1 who dwells with his senses unrestrained he applies himself to such restraint he sets a guard over the sense doors attains restraint thereof that is how a monk has the doors of the sense faculty's garden and how is among moderate in eating herein a monk takes his food thoughtfully and prudently not for sport not for indulgence not for personal charm or adornment but just enough for the support for the continuance of the body for its resting unharmed to help the living of the holy life with this thought my former feeling i checked and i set going no new feeling thus maintenance shall be mine blamelessness and comfort in life thus is among moderate in eating and how is among given to watchfulness or wakefulness by day a monk walks up and down and then sits thus cleansing his heart of conditions that should be checked by night for the first watch he does likewise in the middle watch of the night lying on his right side he takes up the lion posture resting 1 foot on the other and thus collected and composed fixes his thoughts on rising up again in the last watch of the night at early dawn he walks up and down then sits and so cleanses his heart of conditions that should be checked that is how among is given to watchfulness possess of these 4 qualities among is incapable of falling away he is near to nibana so in this we see the the buddha is stressing on 4 practices 4 very important practices which if among uh practices then he is not likely to fall away that means to this rope or to break the precepts et cetera and the major precepts and these 4 uh practices um the first 1 is perfect in virtue second 1 guarding the doors of the sense faculties third 1 moderation and eating fourth 1 devoted to watchfulness or wakefulness i think some of you if you were here in the previous suta reading you had heard the buddha talking about the sure course the sure way and 3 practices were mentioned in there and those 3 practices are also mentioned here namely guarding the doors of the sense faculties moderation in eating and given to wakefulness or watchfulness so these 3 very important practices which make up what is known as charana the buddha is said to be perfect in knowledge and conduct fija charana sampano and in this conduct rana there's a series of practices which make up the holy life and these 3 the buddha always points out as being very important namely the guarding of the sense doors moderation in eating and devotion to wakefulness and these the first 1 mentioned in
commentary: this suta is perfect in virtue that means for a manga to be restrained by the patimoka and he sees danger in the slightest faults and he trains himself in the precepts that is perfect in virtue and guarding the sense doors among seeing an object with the eye does not grasp at the general details or the at the general features or the details thereof this uh i mentioned before this word grass means to take up to seize or even just to accept so in other words what the buddha is saying is that the sixth sense doors we try not to take notice of the objects of the sixth sense doors rather to go into our mind because it is mentioned elsewhere that especially the 5 sense doors of the world uh mara's playground if we go out and notice the things in the [Music] world then we are likely to be caught by mara so that is guarding the sense doors and then the other 1 is moderation in eating uh we eat just enough to support our body not to make it beautiful not to make it uh charming etc or adorning the body and then the last 1 is given to wakefulness the first watch meaning uh normally yeah it's like 6 p.m to 10 p.m that's the first watch and then the buddha says he should sit sit in meditation or walk up and down checking the mind of unwholesome states trying to get rid of unwholesome states in the mind and to develop wholesome states in the mind and similarly for the middle watch up no for the middle watch of the 9 he takes a wrestler uh middle watch the night normally meaning 10 pm to 2 am and then the third watch is 2 am to 6 pm he gets up and he practices meditation and walking meditation and then the rest of the day also he's supposed to be practicing sitting and walking so these 4 things are said by the buddha to be very important [Music] now we come to another suta

items: guarded as to the doors of the sense faculties
